info.name: ESTORE_API_Testing
info.description:
# eStore API — Test Suite

**Base URL**: `{{baseUrl}}` ([https://fakestoreapi.com](https://fakestoreapi.com))  
**Total Requests**: 26  
**Coverage**: Products · Users · Authentication · Carts

---

## What This Collection Tests

End-to-end API test coverage for [FakeStore API](https://fakestoreapi.com) —  
a REST API simulating a real-world e-commerce backend. The suite validates  
functional correctness, response contract integrity, and negative/edge case  
handling across all four resource domains.

---

## Test Design Principles

- **Schema-first validation** — Every success response is validated against  
    a JSON Schema using AJV (Ajv v8, strict mode, allErrors). Type mismatches,  
    missing fields, and unexpected properties all fail the test.
    
- **Pre-request isolation** — Test data is set in `Pre-request Scripts` and  
    cleaned up in `Tests` via `pm.variables.unset()`. No test pollutes the  
    variable scope for the next.
    
- **Negative case coverage** — Every resource (Products, Users, Carts) has  
    corresponding negative tests: non-existent IDs, invalid payloads, and  
    invalid credentials.
    
- **Response time assertions** — All GET endpoints assert response time  
    below 1500ms, establishing a performance baseline.
    

---

## Collection Variables

| Variable | Value | Purpose |
| --- | --- | --- |
| `baseUrl` | [https://fakestoreapi.com](https://fakestoreapi.com) | Base URL for all requests |
| `expectedProductCount` | 20 | Expected total products in catalogue |
| `expectedUsersCount` | 10 | Expected total registered users |
| `countOfAllCarts` | (set at runtime) | Captured from Get All Carts response |
| `Cart7Prdt18Qty` | (set at runtime) | Quantity of product 18 in cart 7, used for update verification |

---

## How to Run

**Postman UI**: Select an environment → Run Collection

**Newman (CLI)**:

``` bash
newman run collections/ESTORE_API_Testing.postman_collection.json \
  -e environments/fakestore.postman_environment.json \
  --reporters cli,htmlextra \
  --reporter-htmlextra-export reports/report.html

 ```

---

## Bugs Discovered

| # | Request | Issue | Severity |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| 1 | Get Non Existing Product | Returns wrong error code (of 200) instead of 404 | Critical |
| 2 | Add cart to Non Existing user | Returns wrong error code (of 201) instead of 404 | Low |
| 3 | Zero quantity updates to existing cart item | At least one quantity item has to be present on cart | High |
| 4 | Add Non Existing Product to a Cart | Returns wrong error code (of 200) instead of 404 | Low |

item.description:
Validates the `/products` resource across full CRUD operations.

Happy path tests cover: retrieving the full catalogue (with array size  
assertion against `expectedProductCount`), fetching a single product by ID  
(with AJV schema validation), creating a new product (with backend-generated  
ID capture), updating an existing product (PUT), and deletion.

Negative tests cover: fetching and deleting a non-existent product ID,  
verifying the API's error handling behaviour.

Variables set by this folder: `testProductId`, `testProductTitle`,  
`testProductCategory`, `newProductId`.

item.name: Users

exec: /*Validate the API response to be a list of users*/
const ajvObj = require('ajv');//import another json validation library
const ajv = new ajvObj({allErrors:true, strict: true, logger: console});//ensure it is strict and logs all errors
pm.test('Is content type returned is JSON', () => {
const responseData = pm.response.json();//extract JSON data from API response
pm.test("Status of API response to get all users", () => {
pm.test('Validate response data against expected structure', () => {
pm.expect(responseData, `Response Data is supposed to be an ARRAY but found to be ${typeof responseData}`).to.be.a('array');//expected response data to be an array
if(!isResponseValid) console.warn('Validation of response data against schema has failed', JSON.stringify(ajv.errors));
pm.expect(isResponseValid, 'Validation of response data against schema has failed').to.be.true;//expected to match the users schema
pm.test("Ensure that Users list returned by API is NOT empty", () => {
pm.expect(responseData.length, `Users List provided by API is of ${responseData.length} size`).to.be.greaterThanOrEqual(usersCount);//ensuring that users count is as expected

item.description:
Validates the `/users` resource and the `/auth/login` endpoint.

Happy path tests cover: retrieving all users (with count assertion against  
`expectedUsersCount`), fetching a user by ID, creating a new user, updating  
a user's password (PUT), and deletion.

Auth tests cover: successful login with valid credentials (JWT token  
captured into `authToken`), and login rejection with invalid credentials.

Negative tests cover: fetching and deleting a non-existent user ID,  
verifying appropriate error responses.

item.name: Carts

exec: /*Validation of carts list returned by API*/
const ajv = new ajvObj({logger: console, strict: true, allErrors: true});//initialized ajv for shema validtion
pm.test('Is content type returned is JSON', () => {
pm.test("Validate that carts list is in expected format", () => {
/*verifying data from API to be in expected format*/
pm.expect(cartList, 'cart list returned by API is NOT an array').to.be.an('array');
if(!isCartListValid) console.warn('cart list returned by API is NOT in the expected format', JSON.stringify(ajv.errors));
pm.expect(isCartListValid, 'cart list returned by API is NOT in the expected format').to.be.true;
pm.test("Verify that cart list is valid array", () => {
pm.expect(cartList, 'cart list returned by API is NOT an array').to.be.an('array');
pm.expect(cartList.length, `cart list is of size ${cartList.length} which is NOT expected`).to.be.greaterThan(0);

item.name: Update Quantity of a Product in CART

exec: /*Data used in request and for response validation*/
/*We are setting quantity 'Cart7Prdt18Qty' as part of API CHAINING of another postman request above*/
/*Verification of API response data for proper quantity updates*/
pm.test("Verifying if the cart update is successful", () => {
pm.test('Is content type returned is JSON', () => {
pm.test("Ensuring the cart id in both request and response is same", () => {
/*verifying cart id is same in both requeset and response*/
pm.expect(requestCartId, 'cartID variable is NOT a number').to.be.a('number');
pm.expect(updatedCart.id, 'there is NO id in the cart provided by API response').to.exist;
pm.expect(responseCartId, 'cart id provided by API is NOT a number').to.be.a('number');
pm.expect(responseCartId, 'cart id in API response is different from id provided in request').to.eql(requestCartId);
pm.test("Verifying product to be present on cart", () => {
/*Checking the product specified in request is presnt in response cart*/
pm.expect(updatedCart.products, 'products in API response is NOT an array').to.be.an('array');
pm.expect(updatedCart.products.length, 'products list in API response is EMPTY').to.be.greaterThan(0);
pm.expect(productIds, `product id in request: ${requestProductId} is NOT present in API response`).to.include.members([requestProductId]);
pm.test("Verifying quanity of product specified to be updated on cart", () => {
/*ensuring that quantity specified in request reflects in API response*/
pm.expect(updatedCart.products, 'products in API response is NOT an array').to.be.an('array');
pm.expect(updatedCart.products.length, 'products list in API response is EMPTY').to.be.greaterThan(0);
pm.expect(filteredProducts.length, `Only one product with product id:${requestProductId} is expected to be in CART but we found either none or more than one`).to.eql(1);
pm.expect(updatedQuantity, `updatedQuantity variable is ${updatedQuantity}. It is required to a valid value for response validation`).to.be.greaterThan(0);
pm.expect(filteredProducts[0].quantity, `Quanity specified in request is NOT matching with API response`).to.eql(updatedQuantity);

item.name: Zero Quantity Update of a Product in CART

exec: /*Data used in request and for response validation*/
/*Verification of API response data for proper quantity updates*/
pm.test("Verifying if the cart update is successful", () => {
pm.test("Ensuring the cart id in both request and response is same", () => {
/*verifying cart id is same in both requeset and response*/
pm.expect(updatedCart.id, 'there is NO id in the cart provided by API response').to.exist;
pm.expect(responseCartId, 'cart id provided by API is NOT a number').to.be.a('number');
pm.expect(responseCartId, 'cart id in API response is different from id provided in request').to.eql(requestCartId);
pm.test("Verifying product to be NOT present on cart", () => {
/*Checking the product specified in request is presnt in response cart*/
pm.expect(updatedCart.products, 'products in API response is NOT an array').to.be.an('array');
pm.expect(productIds, `product id in request: ${requestProductId} is expected NOT to be present in API response`).to.not.contain(requestProductId);

request.description: This Postman Request is to DELETE a invalid / non existent CART

item.description:
Validates the `/carts` resource — the most complex domain in this suite.

Happy path tests cover: retrieving all carts (total count captured into  
`countOfAllCarts`), fetching a cart by ID, creating a new cart for a user,  
adding a product to an existing cart (PUT), updating product quantity in a  
cart (with before/after quantity comparison using `Cart7Prdt18Qty`), zero  
quantity update, and cart deletion.

Negative tests cover: creating a cart for a non-existent user, updating a  
cart with an invalid product ID, and deleting a non-existent cart — all  
asserting appropriate 4xx error responses.
